op.name: isfortran
op.notes: Return True if array is Fortran contiguous.
op.summary: Check if the array is Fortran contiguous but *not* C contiguous.

blocks.text: Check if the array is Fortran contiguous but *not* C contiguous.

This function is obsolete. If you only want to check if an array is Fortran
contiguous use ``a.flags.f_contiguous`` instead.

desc_blocks.text: Returns True if the array is Fortran contiguous but *not* C contiguous.

blocks.text:
np.array allows to specify whether the array is written in C-contiguous
order (last index varies the fastest), or FORTRAN-contiguous order in
memory (first index varies the fastest).

blocks.code:
>>> import numpy as np
>>> a = np.array([[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]], order='C')
>>> a
array([[1, 2, 3],
       [4, 5, 6]])
>>> np.isfortran(a)
False

blocks.text: The transpose of a C-ordered array is a FORTRAN-ordered array.

blocks.code:
>>> a = np.array([[1, 2, 3], [4, 5, 6]], order='C')
>>> a
array([[1, 2, 3],
       [4, 5, 6]])
>>> np.isfortran(a)
False
>>> b = a.T
>>> b
array([[1, 4],
       [2, 5],
       [3, 6]])
>>> np.isfortran(b)
True

blocks.text: C-ordered arrays evaluate as False even if they are also FORTRAN-ordered.
